features.summary: AMD reported record Q1 2022 results with revenue of $5.9 billion (up 71% YoY), including six weeks of Xilinx contribution following the February acquisition close. Excluding Xilinx, organic AMD revenue grew 55% to $5.3 billion. The company saw strong performance across all segments, with server revenue more than doubling YoY for the eighth time in ten quarters, client revenue growing double-digits, and semi-custom revenue up significantly driven by console demand. Gross margin expanded to 53% including Xilinx, or 51% excluding Xilinx.
features.management_outlook: AMD raised full-year 2022 revenue guidance to approximately $26.3 billion (up ~60% YoY), significantly higher than the original 31% organic growth guidance. The increase reflects stronger organic growth now expected in the mid-30% range, driven by robust server and semi-custom demand, plus the full-year addition of Xilinx. Management expects Q2 revenue of ~$6.5 billion (up 69% YoY) and maintains confidence in continued server market share gains with upcoming Genoa processors launching in H2 2022.
features.risks: AMD faces supply chain constraints, particularly in mature node wafers (16nm and above) and substrates, though the company has been investing heavily to secure capacity. The PC market is experiencing softness with management now modeling a high-single digit decline for the full year versus earlier expectations of flat to low-single digit decline. China COVID lockdowns have caused some customer build delays, though AMD hasn't seen significant direct impact on its own supply chain.
features.key_drivers: Server CPU business remains the primary growth driver with continued market share gains and strong cloud/enterprise adoption of EPYC processors. Semi-custom gaming console business is expected to achieve record results in 2022 with strong holiday demand anticipated. The Xilinx acquisition adds diversified revenue streams across data center, communications, automotive and embedded markets, with management identifying significant product and revenue synergies. The pending Pensando acquisition will further expand data center solutions capabilities with proven DPU technology.
